200.description: Successful request. Returns the canonical domain name (CNAME record) of the account

get.description: Get information about origin groups in the project
get.summary: Get a list of origin groups

200.description: Successful request. Returns a list of groups and origins

post.description: Add domains from which the CDN resource will fetch content

201.description: Successful request. Returns the data array of the created group and its origins

get.description: Get information about an origin group by its ID

200.description: Successful request. Returns the data array of the created group and its origins

404.description: Group not found in this account

put.description: Edit an origin group by its ID

200.description: Successful request. The origin group has been modified

delete.description: Delete an existing origin group by its ID

204.description: Successful request. The origin group has been deleted

get.description: Get information about CDN resources in the project
get.summary: Get a list of CDN resources

200.description: Successful request. Returns a list of groups and origins

post.description: Create a new CDN resource in the project

200.description: Successful request. Returns the full list of parameters of the created CDN resource

get.description: Get information about a CDN resource by its ID

200.description: Successful request. Returns the CDN resource data

put.description: Edit a CDN resource by its ID

200.description: Successful request. The CDN resource has been edited

delete.description: Delete a CDN resource by its ID

post.description: Pre-load files to the CDN resource before a user request. Recommended for files larger than 200 MB. No more than one request per minute can be executed

parameters.description: ID of the CDN resource where content should be pre-loaded
parameters.name: resource_id
body
parameters.description: Paths to the files to be loaded, without specifying the domain

201.description: Successful request. The files will be added to the waiting list. The execution time depends on the number of files and their size

post.description: Purge cache from CDN resources to update CDN content. No more than one request per minute can be executed

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Paths to the files to be deleted:
- `[]` — delete all files on the CDN resource.
- path template — delete specific files. Use the `*` operator to replace any number of characters in the path.
- file path without domain name — purge the cache of the selected file

201.description: Successful request. The specified files will be deleted

post.description: The request allows issuing a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate for a resource. The Let's Encrypt certificate will be issued for all CNAME records of the CDN resource. After applying the certificate to the resource, HTTPS will be enabled automatically. The certificate will be applied to all new CNAME records of the CDN resource and will be renewed automatically. To disable automatic certificate renewal, change the `ssl_automate` parameter

201.description: Successful request. The certificate issuance process has started and may take up to 30 minutes

400.description:
Invalid request parameters specified. Error options:
- **Resource already has automated certificate** — the specified resource already has a certificate. Verify that the resource ID is correct. If a new certificate is needed, first revoke the current one, then repeat the request.
- **Certificate issuing process have already being launched** — the certificate issuance process has started and may take up to 30 minutes. Wait for it to complete.
- **We cannot issue a Let's Encrypt certificate for a resource with a wildcard cname** — the specified resource has an additional CNAME with a wildcard character. Only a custom certificate can be used for such a resource

post.description: Revoke and stop auto-renewal of a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate for a CDN resource

parameters.description: Certificate deletion options: `true` — the certificate will be detached from all resources where it is used and deleted, `false` — the certificate will be detached only from the specified resource
parameters.name: force

400.description:
Invalid request parameters specified. Error options:
- **Resource doesn't have any certificates** — the specified resource has no certificates. Verify that the resource ID is correct.
- **Resource doesn't support automatic renewal** — the specified resource has no Let’s Encrypt certificates. Verify that the resource ID is correct.
- **Several resources [123, 124, 125] are linked to this certificate. Use the ?force=true query param to revoke certificate from several resources** — the certificate connected to the specified resource is also used by other resources. To revoke the certificate from all resources, specify the `?force=true` parameter

get.description: Get information about all SSL certificates in the project

200.description: Successful request. Returns a list of all certificates with information about them

post.description: Add an SSL certificate for delivering content via HTTPS protocol

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request body must contain all certificate lines and private keys. Each certificate is written on a separate line. Use `\n` to separate lines in the request body

201.description: SSL certificate successfully installed. The response will return information about the installed certificate

delete.description: Delete an SSL certificate by its ID

patch.description: Modify an SSL certificate by its ID

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request body contains the parameters to be changed

200.description: SSL certificate parameters have been successfully changed. The response will return a list of all current SSL certificate parameters

parameters.description: Service name. Specify CDN in this parameter
parameters.name: service

parameters.description: Start date of the reporting period in ISO 8061/RFC 3339 format, UTC. It is preferable not to specify minutes and seconds. Examples: `2018-11-01T00:00:00.000`, `2018-11-01`
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: End date of the reporting period in ISO 8061/RFC 3339 format, UTC. It is preferable not to specify minutes and seconds. Examples: `2018-11-01T00:00:00.000`, `2018-11-01`
parameters.name: to

parameters.description: CDN resource ID. You can specify multiple IDs by listing them with `&`. Example: `resource=1&resource=2`
parameters.name: resource

parameters.description:
Specifies the metric for which statistics should be obtained:
- **upstream_bytes** — traffic in bytes from the origin server to CDN servers or to the shield server;
- **sent_bytes** — traffic in bytes from the CDN server to clients;
- **shield_bytes** — traffic in bytes from the shield server to CDN servers;
- **total_bytes** — total data for the **shield_bytes**, **upstream_bytes**, and **sent_bytes** parameters;
- **requests** — number of requests to edge servers;
- **responses_2xx** — number of responses with 2xx codes;
- **responses_3xx** — number of responses with 3xx codes;
- **responses_4xx** — number of responses with 4xx codes;
- **responses_5xx** — number of responses with 5xx codes;
- **responses_hit** — number of responses with the `Cache: HIT` header;
- **responses_miss** — number of responses with the `Cache: MISS` header;
- **cache_hit_traffic_ratio** — share of non-cacheable traffic in the total volume: `1 - upstream_bytes / sent_bytes`;
- **95_percentile** — bandwidth indicator that cuts off 5% of bandwidth peaks;
- **min_bandwidth** — minimum bandwidth indicator;
- **max_bandwidth** — maximum bandwidth indicator
parameters.name: metrics

200.description: Successful request. Returns data on the specified metrics

204.description: No data for the specified period

get.description: The request allows to obtain statistics for a CDN server for the last 90 days
get.summary: Get statistics for a CDN resource

parameters.description: Start date of the reporting period in ISO 8061/RFC 3339 format, UTC. It is preferable not to specify minutes and seconds. Examples: `2018-11-01T00:00:00.000`, `2018-11-01`
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: End date of the reporting period in ISO 8061/RFC 3339 format, UTC. It is preferable not to specify minutes and seconds. Examples: `2018-11-01T00:00:00.000`, `2018-11-01`
parameters.name: to

parameters.description: Time intervals by which data will be detailed. Available values: 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 1d
parameters.name: granularity

parameters.description:
Specifies the metric for which statistics should be obtained:
- **upstream_bytes** — traffic in bytes from the origin server to CDN servers or to the shield server;
- **sent_bytes** — traffic in bytes from the CDN server to clients;
- **shield_bytes** — traffic in bytes from the shield server to CDN servers;
- **total_bytes** — total data for the **shield_bytes**, **upstream_bytes**, and **sent_bytes** parameters;
- **cdn_bytes** — total data for the **sent_bytes** and **shield_bytes** parameters;
- **requests_waf_passed** — number of requests passing through the Basic WAF option;
- **requests** — number of requests to edge servers;
- **responses_2xx** — number of responses with 2xx codes;
- **responses_3xx** — number of responses with 3xx codes;
- **responses_4xx** — number of responses with 4xx codes;
- **responses_5xx** — number of responses with 5xx codes;
- **response_types** — response statistics by content type;
- **responses_hit** — number of responses with the `Cache: HIT` header;
- **responses_miss** — number of responses with the `Cache: MISS` header;
- **cache_hit_traffic_ratio** — ratio of responses with the `Cache: HIT` header to the total number of requests: `responses_hit / requests`;
- **shield_traffic_ratio** — efficiency of Origin Shielding: how much more traffic passes through the shield server compared to a regular origin. Calculated using the formula: `(shield_bytes - upstream_bytes) / shield_bytes`;
- **image_processed** — number of images optimized through the image optimization service
parameters.name: metrics

parameters.description:
Report data grouping (multiple values can be specified):
- **resource** — by resource ID;
- **region** — by CDN server region;
- **country** — by CDN server country;
- **vhost** — by resource CNAME
parameters.name: group_by

parameters.description: Countries for which statistics should be displayed. Uses the short name in English from the ISO 3166 standard
parameters.name: countries

parameters.description:
Specifies the regions for which statistics should be displayed. Available values:
- **na** — North America;
- **eu** — Europe;
- **cis** — Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS);
- **asia** — Asia;
- **au** — Australia;
- **latam** — Latin America;
- **me** — Middle East;
- **africa** — Africa;
- **sa** — South America;
- **ru** — Russia.
parameters.name: regions

parameters.description: CDN resource. To specify multiple resources, use `&`
parameters.name: resource

200.description: Successful request. Returns data on the specified metrics

204.description: No data for the specified period

name.description: Unique name of the SSL certificate

sslPrivateKey.description: Private key of the SSL certificate

backup.description: Indicator of using the origin as a backup: `true` — the origin will be used when one of the active origins becomes unavailable

enabled.description: Origin availability: `true` — origin is available, `false` — origin is not available

source.description: IP address or domain name of the origin and port, if configured

active.description: Resource activity: `true` — resource is active, `false` — resource is inactive

enabled.description: Resource availability: `true` — resource is available, `false` — resource is unavailable

logTarget.description: IP address or domain name and port (host:port) where logs will be sent in real time. To receive logs, configure your server to receive logs via syslog

origin.description: IP address or domain name of the origin and port, if configured. You can use either the 'origin' or 'originGroup' parameter in the request

originGroup.description: ID or name of the origin group. You can use either the 'origin' or 'originGroup' parameter in the request

sslData.description: Specify the ID of the SSL certificate to be used for the CDN resource. The parameter will be filled in automatically if you issue a Let's Encrypt certificate. The parameter can only be used with `sslEnabled: true`

sslEnabled.description: Indicator of using the HTTPS protocol: `true` — HTTPS protocol is used for content delivery, `false` — HTTPS protocol is not used for content delivery

ssl_automated.description: Indicator of automatic certificate renewal: `true` — Let's Encrypt SSL certificate is renewed automatically upon expiration, `false` — Let's Encrypt SSL certificate is not renewed upon expiration (content will not be delivered via HTTPS). The parameter can only be used with `sslEnabled: true`

backup.description: Indicator of using the origin as a backup: `true` — the origin will be used when one of the active origins becomes unavailable

enabled.description: Origin availability: `true` — origin is available, `false` — origin is not available

source.description: IP address or domain name of the origin and port, if configured

cname.description: CNAME that will be used for content delivery via CDN

backup.description: Indicator of using the origin as a backup: `true` — the origin will be used when one of the active origins becomes unavailable

enabled.description: Origin availability: `true` — origin is available, `false` — origin is not available

source.description: IP address or domain name of the origin and port, if configured

active.description: Resource activity: `true` — resource is active, `false` — resource is inactive

cname.description: Alias used for content delivery via CDN

deleted.description: Resource deletion indicator: `true` — the resource has been deleted, `false` — the resource has not been deleted

enabled.description: Resource availability: `true` — resource is available, content is delivered, `false` — resource is not available, users receive a `403` response

preset_applied.description: Indicator of using preset settings on the CDN resource: `true` — use preset settings, CDN resource options cannot be changed, `false` — do not use preset settings

proxy_ssl_enabled.description: SSL validation of the origin before establishing a connection: `true` — CDN checks the validity of the origin's SSL certificate before establishing a connection, `false` — CDN does not check the origin's SSL certificate

shielded.description: Indicator of origin shielding: `true` — origin shielding is enabled, `false` — origin shielding is not enabled

sslData.description: Specify the ID of the SSL certificate to be used for the CDN resource. The parameter will be filled in automatically if you issue a Let's Encrypt certificate

sslEnabled.description: Indicator of using the HTTPS protocol: `true` — HTTPS protocol is used for content delivery, `false` — HTTPS protocol is not used for content delivery

ssl_automated.description: Indicator of automatic certificate renewal: `true` — Let's Encrypt SSL certificate is renewed automatically upon expiration, `false` — Let's Encrypt SSL certificate is not renewed upon expiration (content will not be delivered via HTTPS). The parameter can only be used with `sslEnabled: true`

status.description: CDN resource status: `active` — resource is ready to use, `suspended` — resource is unavailable because the service is suspended, `processed` — resource is being processed and is not yet ready to use

vp_enabled.description: CDN resource integration status with the streaming platform: `true` — resource is configured for the streaming platform, changing resource settings may affect its operation; `false` — resource is not integrated with the streaming platform

automated.description: Indicator of automatic certificate renewal: `true` — Let's Encrypt SSL certificate is renewed automatically upon expiration, `false` — Let's Encrypt SSL certificate is not renewed upon expiration (content will not be delivered via HTTPS). The parameter can only be used with `sslEnabled: true`

cert_issuer.description: Name of the certification authority issuing the SSL certificate

cert_subject_cn.description: Domain protected by the SSL certificate

deleted.description: Certificate deletion indicator: `true` — the certificate has been deleted and cannot be used for a CDN resource, `false` — the certificate is in use

hasRelatedResources.description: Certificate usage indicator: `true` — the certificate is used for CDN resources, `false` — the certificate is not in use

validity_not_after.description: Date when the certificate will become untrusted

validity_not_before.description: Date when the certificate will become valid

95_percentile.description: Bandwidth indicator that cuts off 5% of bandwidth peaks

cache_hit_traffic_ratio.description: Share of non-cacheable traffic in the total volume: `1 - upstream_bytes / sent_bytes`

requests.description: Number of requests to edge servers

responses_miss.description: Number of responses with the `Cache: MISS` header

shield_bytes.description: Traffic in bytes from the shield server to CDN servers

sent_bytes.description: Traffic in bytes from the CDN server to clients

upstream_bytes.description: Traffic in bytes from the origin server to CDN servers or to the shield server

total_bytes.description: Total data for the `shield_bytes`, `upstream_bytes`, and `sent_bytes` parameters

origin_ids.description: Array with data about all origins in the group, including their IDs

origins.description: Array with data about all origins in the group, without their IDs

useNext.description: Use the next origin in case of an error: `true` — if this origin responds with `4XX` or `5XX` errors, the next one in the list will be used

origins.description: Data of all IP addresses or domain names being added to the group

useNext.description: Use the next origin in case of an error: `true` — if this origin responds with `4XX` or `5XX` errors, the next one in the list will be used

origins.description: Data of all IP addresses or domain names being added to the group

useNext.description: Use the next origin in case of an error: `true` — if this origin responds with `4XX` or `5XX` errors, the next one in the list will be used

originProtocolEnum.description: Protocol for communication with the origin

name.description: New name of the SSL certificate. Must be unique

allowedHttpMethods.description: HTTP methods available for the CDN resource

enabled.description: HTTP methods availability: `true` — HTTP methods are available for the resource, `false` — HTTP methods are not available for the resource
Usage indicator: `true` — Brotli compression is used, `false` — not used

edge_cache_settings.description: Caching settings on the CDN resource

custom_values.description: Caching time in seconds for specific response codes

default.description: Caching time in seconds, controlled by the origin. Used for HTTP 200, 201, 204, 206, 301, 302, 303, 304, 307, 308 status codes if the origin server does not have caching HTTP headers. Responses with other codes will not be cached

fetch_compressed.description: Compression at the origin. The option reduces bandwidth between the origin server and cache servers and increases content delivery speed by reducing file compression time on the CDN side. CDN will fetch already compressed content from the origin server, so the origin must support compression. In this case, users will receive compressed content

body.description: URL or text of the response code

ignoreQueryString.description: Ignore query parameters when caching files

value.description: Ignoring type: `true` — all query parameters are ignored, `false` — parameters specified in `query_params_blacklist` are ignored, or all parameters except those specified in `query_params_whitelist` are ignored

query_params_blacklist.description: The option allows CDN to ignore the specified parameters and cache them as separate objects. The `ignoreQueryString`, `query_params_whitelist`, and `query_params_blacklist` options cannot be enabled simultaneously

query_params_whitelist.description: The option allows CDN to ignore all parameters except the specified ones and cache them as separate objects. The `ignoreQueryString`, `query_params_whitelist`, and `query_params_blacklist` options cannot be enabled simultaneously

value.description: List of parameters that cannot be ignored

country_acl.description: Allows protecting content from access from certain countries

enabled.description: Usage indicator: `true` — option is enabled, `false` — option is not enabled

policy_type.description: Policy type: `allow` — allow access to all countries except those specified in the `excepted_values` parameter; `deny` — deny access to all countries except those specified in the `excepted_values` parameter

excepted_values.description: List of countries in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format

referrer_acl.description: Setting allows protecting content from being published on other sites

enabled.description: Usage indicator: `true` — option is enabled, `false` — option is not enabled

policy_type.description: Policy type: `allow` — allow access to all domains except those specified in the `excepted_values` parameter; `deny` — deny access to all domains except those specified in the `excepted_values` parameter

excepted_values.description: List of domains or domain masks without `http://` or `https://`

ip_address_acl.description: Setting allows denying access to content for certain IP addresses

enabled.description: Usage indicator: `true` — option is enabled, `false` — option is not enabled

policy_type.description: Policy type: `allow` — allow access to IP addresses except those specified in the `excepted_values` parameter; `deny` — deny access to all IP addresses except those specified in the `excepted_values` parameter

excepted_values.description: List of IP addresses with subnet mask. The access policy works based on network addresses calculated from the specified IP addresses. If two or more IP addresses belong to the same network, it is sufficient to specify only one of these IP addresses

slice.description: Optimization of large file delivery. The option accelerates caching of large files. The file will be requested and stored in cache in 10 MB parts

stale.description: Always online: if the origin server is unavailable, outdated content will be delivered from cache

staticHeaders.description: HTTP headers that the CDN server will add to the response to the client

staticRequestHeaders.description: HTTP headers that CDN servers will add to requests to the origin server

websockets.description: Setting allows WebSocket connections to the origin

paths.description: Paths to files that need to be pre-loaded on the CDN resource
Path to files to be deleted

seriesMetrics.description: Details of the requested parameters. Each parameter has two values: the first is the UNIX timestamp when the statistics were obtained, and the second is the metric value

cache_hit_requests_ratio.description: Volume of cacheable content delivered. Calculated using the formula: `responses_hit / requests`

cache_hit_traffic_ratio.description: Share of non-cacheable traffic in the total volume. Calculated using the formula: `1 - upstream_bytes / sent_bytes`

cdn_bytes.description: Sum of traffic from shield servers (`shield_bytes`) to CDN servers and from CDN servers to clients (`sent_bytes`)

image_processed.description: Number of images optimized through the image optimization service

requests.description: Number of requests to edge servers

requests_waf_passed.description: Number of requests passing through the Basic WAF option

responses_hit.description: Number of responses with the `Cache: HIT` header

responses_miss.description: Number of responses with the `Cache: MISS` header

sent_bytes.description: Traffic in bytes from the CDN server to clients

shield_bytes.description: Traffic in bytes from the shield server to CDN servers

shield_traffic_ratio.description: Efficiency of Origin Shielding: how much more traffic passes through the shield server compared to a regular origin. Calculated using the formula: `(shield_bytes - upstream_bytes) / shield_bytes`

total_bytes.description: Total data for the `shield_bytes`, `upstream_bytes`, and `sent_bytes` parameters

upstream_bytes.description: Traffic in bytes from the origin server to CDN servers or to the shield server
